title: T-E formulation-based modeling of thin HTS shell magnetization
abstract: Numerical methods for modeling thin-film magnetization are primarily focused on computing the current density distribution. The highly nonlinear current-voltage characteristic of type-II superconductors significantly complicates the accurate computation of the electric field. The T-E formulation-based mixed finite element method, previously derived for flat superconducting films, enables the simultaneous, accurate determination of both variables. Another advantage of this method is that the computational domain is limited to the film itself: no meshing of the surrounding space is required. The thin-shell approximation reduces the problem to a two-dimensional one.   This work extends the T-E formulation and numerical method to non-flat superconducting shells with a metal substrate. We validate the method with several test examples, including modeling the magnetization of a sphere. The method is then applied to a realistic model of a cylindrical magnetic dynamo pump, and the generated open-circuit voltage is computed.
summary: This paper presents an improved numerical framework for simulating the electromagnetic behavior of curved, thin-film superconductors. By using a mixed T-E formulation, the authors achieve higher accuracy in calculating electric fields in highly nonlinear regimes, which is critical for designing devices like superconducting dynamo pumps.
main_problem: Developing an accurate numerical method for modeling the magnetization and electric field of non-flat, thin high-temperature superconducting (HTS) shells, specifically overcoming errors in electric field computation caused by highly nonlinear current-voltage characteristics.
main_result: The extended T-E formulation-based mixed finite element method provides superior electric field accuracy compared to direct methods and successfully models complex geometries like spheres and cylindrical magnetic dynamo pumps.
method: A mixed finite element method using nonconforming Crouzeix-Raviart elements for the T-potential and piecewise constant vector functions for the rotated electric field, utilizing both analytical and Gauss quadrature integration.
model_or_system: Thin-shell approximation of HTS layers (e.g., REBCO/YBCO) on metal substrates, modeled with a power-law resistivity and field-dependent critical current density in geometries such as spheres, hemispheres, and cylinders.
key_observables: Sheet current density (j), electric field (e), magnetic induction (B), and generated open-circuit voltage (V).
important_parameters: Power-law index (n), critical sheet current density (jcs), substrate resistivity (rho_m), and external magnetic field (h).
main_assumptions: The superconducting film is treated as infinitely thin, the surface is orientable, and the magnetic field calculation uses a piecewise constant approximation of the current-induced field.
figures_summary: The figures show triangular meshes for various geometries (hemisphere, sphere, cylinder), comparison plots of finite element solutions against spectral reference methods, and current density distributions in a dynamo pump.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the mathematical derivation of the T-E formulation for non-flat shells, moves to the numerical discretization and implementation details, validates the method against spectral methods for simple geometries, and concludes with a realistic application to a cylindrical magnetic dynamo pump.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
